target.name: Einride
target.founder: Robert Falck, Filip Lilja, Linnéa Kornehed

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Einride is a transportation-sector company at the public stage with a current valuation of $1.8B and total capital raised exceeding $752M. The comp universe in transportation remains sparse at this valuation tier; five private peers were identified including Rivian, The Boring Company, Harbinger, REGENT Craft, and Vertical Aerospace. No public comparables exist in the narrowly defined peer set, limiting traditional equity market benchmarking.

A peer-median valuation approach was not applicable due to insufficient depth in the narrow peer cohort. Transportation yielded only four same-sector peers, with fewer than three datapoints satisfying filters for stage alignment, adjacent-stage positioning, or similar valuation tier. Applying an all-sector median would risk material distortion given the sector's capital intensity and commercialization dependencies unique to transportation infrastructure plays.

The analysis does not produce a triangulated valuation range given the inapplicability of the primary method and absence of public market anchors. Key uncertainties center on the definition of true comparability within transportation—stage progression, technology maturity, and path to unit economics vary substantially across autonomous vehicles, micromobility, and aerospace verticals. Further valuation work would require either identifying additional same-stage private transactions or developing bottom-up models anchored to fleet deployment timelines and margin trajectories specific to Einride's autonomous freight segment.
